title: Distributed Systems
description: Advanced study of distributed systems covering consistency models, distributed consensus, fault tolerance, and distributed data storage. Topics include clock synchronization, logical clocks, distributed mutual exclusion, consensus protocols (Paxos, Raft), distributed hash tables, MapReduce, and microservices communication patterns. Prerequisites include CS303 (OS concepts for process management and concurrency) and CS304 (networking for distributed communication). Students implement distributed system components and analyze their correctness and performance properties.
outcomes: Analyze distributed system correctness using formal models
Implement consensus protocols for distributed agreement
Apply consistency models to distributed storage systems
Implement MapReduce programs for large-scale data processing
Evaluate tradeoffs in distributed system design (CAP theorem)
